finding.description: The search method uses raw SQL queries, which can lead to SQL injection vulnerabilities.
finding.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-01] Raw SQL query — SQL injection vulnerability
finding.recommendation: Use Eloquent ORM methods instead of raw SQL queries.
tests: Test that the search method is secure against SQL injection.

finding.description: The Vendor model uses $fillable instead of $guarded, which violates the convention for mass assignment protection.
finding.recommendation: Change the model to use $guarded = ['id']; instead of $fillable.
tests: Test that mass assignment works correctly with the new guarded setting.
finding.notes: Refer to rule CTDL-02 for mass assignment conventions.

finding.description: The search view uses {!! !!} for outputting vendor names, which can lead to XSS vulnerabilities if not properly sanitized.
finding.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-03] Unsafe output in search view
finding.recommendation: Change {!! $vendor->name !!} to {{ $vendor->name }} to ensure proper escaping.
tests: Test that the output is properly escaped and does not allow XSS.
finding.notes: Refer to rule CTDL-03 for XSS prevention.

finding.description: The store method does not validate the request data.
tests: Test that invalid data returns a validation error.
Test that valid data creates a purchase order.

finding.description: The Vendor model is missing the HasCreator and ActivityLogged traits, which are required for auditing and tracking.
finding.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-07] Missing HasCreator trait in Vendor model
finding.recommendation: Implement the HasCreator and ActivityLogged traits in the Vendor model.
tests: Test that the model correctly tracks creator and activity logs.
finding.notes: Refer to rules CTDL-07 and CTDL-08 for auditing requirements.

finding.description: The Vendor model is missing the explicit definition of the $table property with the escm_ prefix, violating the convention.
finding.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-10] Missing $table property in Vendor model
finding.recommendation: Add the property $table = 'escm_vendors'; to the Vendor model.
tests: Test that the model correctly references the database table.
finding.notes: Refer to rule CTDL-10 for table naming conventions.

finding.description: The index method does not call checkAccessPermission() and lacks scopeProject() for multi-tenant isolation.
finding.recommendation: Add checkAccessPermission() and ESCM::scopeProject(auth()->user()) to the index method.
tests: Test that unauthorized access is blocked.
Test that only relevant purchase orders are returned based on user permissions.

finding.description: The store method logs sensitive data without masking.
finding.check_name: SAST | [WIKA-Q08] Logging sensitive data without masking
finding.recommendation: Mask sensitive fields like password and pin before logging.
tests: Test that sensitive data is not logged.

fp_findings.description: The store method in VendorController does not validate the incoming request data, which can lead to invalid data being saved to the database.
fp_findings.check_name: SAST | [WIKA-Q02] Missing validation in store method
fp_findings.recommendation: Add validation to the store method using $request->validate([...]) to ensure data integrity.
tests: Test that invalid data returns a validation error.
Test that valid data is saved correctly.
fp_findings.notes: Refer to rule WIKA-Q02 for validation requirements.

fp_findings.description: The search method uses a raw SQL query, which violates the convention of using Eloquent ORM for database access.
fp_findings.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-01] Raw SQL query in search method
fp_findings.recommendation: Replace the raw SQL query with an Eloquent query using the Vendor model.
tests: Test that the search functionality works correctly with Eloquent.
Test for SQL injection vulnerabilities.
fp_findings.notes: Refer to rule CTDL-01 for ORM usage.

fp_findings.description: The export method does not check for user permissions before exporting vendor data, which can lead to unauthorized access.
fp_findings.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-11] Missing permission check in export method
fp_findings.recommendation: Add a permission check at the start of the export method using checkAccessPermission().
tests: Test that unauthorized users cannot access the export functionality.
Test that authorized users can successfully export data.
fp_findings.notes: Refer to rule CTDL-11 for authorization checks.

fp_findings.description: The detail method does not follow the Citadel page lifecycle.
fp_findings.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-06] Does not follow Citadel Page lifecycle
fp_findings.recommendation: Follow the Citadel page lifecycle in the detail method.
tests: Test that the detail page renders correctly.

fp_findings.description: The syncFromSap method does not extend the Base sync service.
fp_findings.check_name: SAST | [WIKA-Q06] Sync service does not extend Base
fp_findings.recommendation: Extend the Base sync service in the syncFromSap method.
tests: Test that the sync service functions correctly.

fp_findings.description: The callback method is vulnerable to open redirects.
fp_findings.recommendation: Validate the redirect URL to prevent open redirects.
tests: Test that invalid redirect URLs are blocked.

findings.description: The search method uses raw SQL queries, which can lead to SQL injection vulnerabilities.
findings.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-01] Raw SQL query — SQL injection vulnerability
findings.recommendation: Use Eloquent ORM methods instead of raw SQL queries.
tests: Test that the search method is secure against SQL injection.

findings.description: The search method also uses DB::raw() which is not allowed unless for complex aggregations.
findings.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-01] Raw SQL query — SQL injection vulnerability
findings.recommendation: Replace DB::raw() with Eloquent ORM methods.
tests: Test that the stats calculation is done securely.

findings.recommendation: Add checkAccessPermission() to the update_status method.
tests: Test that unauthorized actions are blocked.
